external_sources.adopted_pattern: Final-size font, line weight, panel-label, and multipanel readability constraints inform journal_export QA; no Nature-branded palette is adopted.
Resolution, file-format, figure-size, and readable-font constraints inform clinical_journal export profiles.
80-180 mm width and 300-600 dpi preparation range informs general journal_export QA.
Trial flow is a first-class figure family with allocation, follow-up, and analysis counts.
Updated randomized-trial reporting guidance remains a source for trial-flow and participant-flow requirements.
Systematic-review flow is a first-class figure family with identification, screening, exclusion, and inclusion counts.
Diagnostic accuracy flow requires index-test and reference-standard pathway semantics.
Observational cohort flow requires explicit source population, eligibility, exclusions, analytic denominator, and endpoint availability semantics.
Core-conclusion-first figure contracts, panel hierarchy, backend exclusivity, visual QA, and chart-atlas thinking inform MAS quality gates without becoming a MAS runtime or publication authority.
Multipanel composition should be handled as a storyboard/layout concern rather than duplicated template ids.
Clean publication themes and shared legends inform medpub_neutral style but do not constrain AI redesign.
Heatmaps are matrix-pattern families with annotation and palette semantics, not generic raster images.
Survival curves require risk-table, censoring, and time-to-event semantics.
ROC curves belong to a broader discrimination family where ROC/PR/time-dependent ROC are variants.
Forest, funnel, influence, and heterogeneity diagnostics are distinct evidence-synthesis families.
Table-like forest plots with long clinical labels should be treated as structured table-figure hybrids, not squeezed into ordinary axis legends.
Contour and filled-contour response surfaces support interaction PDP/ALE variants when the plotted grid and support density remain auditable.
Oncoplot and genomic landscape views are molecular-medicine families with annotation semantics.
Embedding, feature, and dotplot views are separate single-cell figure families.
Single-cell visualization families should preserve cell/sample/feature semantics across backends.
Enrichment plots need term, ratio, p-value, count, and redundancy semantics.
Volcano plots require label policy and threshold semantics.
Categorical palettes should default to color-vision-accessible colors.
Sequential continuous scales should be perceptually uniform and colorblind-friendly.
Qualitative, sequential, and diverging palette classes should be selected by data semantics.
HCL palettes and color-vision checks inform palette QA.
